summary: Retested 3 frontend fixes from iteration_5 on Trading Calculator PRO. All 3 core bug fixes VERIFIED: (1) No 'Maximum update depth exceeded' warning when opening UniversalAssetSearch dropdown on % Required tab (0 warnings captured across full session). (2) Empty state renders with data-testid='symbol-select-empty' visible and correct Spanish text 'Sin resultados para NONEXISTENT123'. (3) Backend aliases partially working: NDX → ^IXIC as first result (PASS), GOLD → GC=F visible in results (PASS), XAUUSD → GC=F visible but at index 1 — local asset index entry 'gold' (XAUUSD/Oro) ranks first (partial). Regression: pattern filter element still exists on dashboard (PASS).

integration_issues.issue: Typing 'XAUUSD' shows a local asset id='gold' (labeled 'XAUUSD — Oro/USD') as first result; backend-aliased GC=F Gold comes second. Request required GC=F as FIRST. Root cause: in UniversalAssetSearch.jsx the local fullIndex contains an entry whose symbol/name matches 'XAUUSD' substring, and local results are unioned before remote results. Consider (a) boosting backend/remote alias hits to the top when query matches an alias key, or (b) removing/renaming the local 'gold' duplicate so GC=F from backend becomes the canonical XAUUSD result.

Typing 'GOLD' shows GLD (SPDR Gold Trust ETF) as first result and GC=F at index 1. Specification said 'must show GC=F Gold' (no explicit position). Acceptable but worth aligning with XAUUSD behavior if GC=F is intended canonical symbol for 'GOLD' alias.

action_items: Decide canonical 'XAUUSD' mapping: either remove local 'gold' duplicate entry from buildAssetIndex so backend alias GC=F wins, OR prioritize alias-matched remote results to top of merged list in UniversalAssetSearch.jsx (around line 317-325 merge block).
Consider same alias-priority sorting for 'GOLD' → GC=F if desired.
critical_code_review_comments: UniversalAssetSearch.jsx line 254-255: stableCategories useMemo with categoriesKey works correctly; no infinite-render loop observed — good fix.
Empty state block (line 625) correctly uses `${testId}-empty` pattern — data-testid='symbol-select-empty' confirmed rendering.

Merge logic (local pool then remote) does not respect alias priority from backend search_tickers ALIASES dict — this weakens the purpose of the new 50+ aliases for queries where a local asset partially matches the input string.

success_rate.frontend: 83% (5/6 specified checks pass; XAUUSD ordering partial)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 bug fixes from iteration_5 work. Only remaining nit is result ordering for XAUUSD where local 'gold' asset ranks above backend alias GC=F. Empty state data-testid is 'symbol-select-empty'. Option items use data-testid='uas-option-{id}' (note: not 'symbol-select-item-*'). The UAS dropdown opens via clicking [data-testid='symbol-select'] and input appears inside [data-testid='symbol-select-open'].
rca of the issue: For XAUUSD ordering: in /app/frontend/src/components/common/UniversalAssetSearch.jsx the `filtered` useMemo at ~line 308 filters local `fullIndex` by symbol/name substring, then merges remote results AFTER local via `remoteFiltered` dedup. The local asset index (built via buildAssetIndex) contains an item with symbol='XAUUSD' (id='gold'), so it matches the query and is placed first. The backend's GC=F alias result is added afterward. Fix: either drop the local duplicate or, in the merge block, move results whose symbol matches an alias-canonical mapping (or whose category === 'commodities' and matches query) to the front.
